name: Cranberry Maple Upside-Down Gingerbread
recipeIngredient: 1 Orange, peeled and cut into chunks
1/4 cup Butter or margarine, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Place cranberries and orange chunks in a food processor; pulse to coarsely chop. Set aside.
2. Melt 3 tablespoons butter in a 9-inch square baking pan at 350°F (175°C) oven. Pour maple syrup over the melted butter.
3. Spoon the cranberry mixture evenly over the maple syrup in the baking pan. Sprinkle with raisins. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking soda, ground cinnamon, ground ginger, ground cloves, and salt.
5. In a blender or food processor, blend buttermilk, butter, sugar, egg, and molasses until smooth, about 2 minutes. Add preserved ginger and blend until mixed.
6. Pour the wet ingredients over the dry ingredients and mix thoroughly.
7. Pour the batter over the cranberry mixture.
8. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 40 to 50 minutes.
9. Loosen the cake from the edges of the pan and invert immediately onto a serving plate.
10. Serve warm topped with a dollop of whipped cream.
